topics.topic: Antimatter is the most expensive substance on Earth, costing roughly $62.5 trillion per gram, or $1.75 quadrillion per ounce.
Mercury and Venus are the only two planets in the solar system that do not have any moons.
The kookaburra is native to Australia and New Guinea; its calls are often used as sound effects in movies for jungles in Africa or South America.
Although there are millions of islands on Earth, only around 11,000 are permanently inhabited by humans.
The odds of giving birth to identical twins is 1 in every 250 pregnancies.
The number 22 wasn’t meant to feature in Joseph Heller’s “Catch-22,” the book or the paradox. It was meant to be “Catch-18,” but a popular book called “Mila 18” had just been published.
In 1797, William Charles Cole Claiborne was elected to the U.S. Congress at 22, despite the minimum age being 25, due to his exceptional qualifications and popularity.
Vehophobia is the intense fear of driving, often developing after car accidents. Studies show that 42-77% of people involved in serious crashes later experience driving-related anxiety.
Monday is the only day of the week with a valid English anagram, where its letters can be rearranged to spell the word “dynamo.”
Los Pinos, once the residence of Mexico’s presidents, is 14 times the size of the White House and became accessible to the public on December 1, 2018.
A hamster’s cheek pouches stretch back to its shoulders, allowing it to carry food nearly equal to its own body weight in a single trip.
June 22 is World Rainforest Day, a day dedicated to raising awareness about the challenges faced by rainforests and encouraging actions to protect these vital ecosystems.
Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan is the world’s largest fully operational launch site, used for both crewed and uncrewed rockets, and leased to Russia.
On March 6, 1957, Ghana became the first sub-Saharan African nation to gain independence from colonial rule.
American bacon usually comes from pork belly, while British back bacon comes from the loin with some belly attached, which makes it leaner than American streaky bacon.
NASA scientists reported in 2015 that Mars once held more water than Earth’s Arctic Ocean, forming a vast ocean that covered up to 19% of the planet’s surface.
An icosidigon is a shape with 22 sides, and it’s primarily used in geometry and educational software to explore properties of high-sided polygons. Drawn to scale, it’s nearly circular.
Certain hospitals in Rwanda can order blood for transfusions and have it delivered via drone.
Tonight, June 22, 2026, the Moon is in its First Quarter phase, meaning it is one quarter of the way through its orbit around Earth.
American actress Meryl Streep is celebrating her 77th birthday today. She was born on June 22, 1949 in New Jersey, USA, making her zodiac sign Cancer.
While the first half of June was seen as an incredibly unlucky time to get married in ancient Rome, the second half was a favorite!
One of South Korea’s worst oil spills occurred on December 7, 2007, and within 33 days, more than one million civilians, soldiers, and officials had volunteered to help with the cleanup.
Badgers obtain the majority of their food by digging underground, with worms making up about 60–80% of a European badger’s diet.
USB stands for Universal Serial Bus. “Universal” means it works with many kinds of devices, “serial” means data is sent in sequence, and “bus” means a shared connection system.
“Daughter from California” syndrome is a phrase used in medicine to describe long-lost relatives who suddenly appear and insist a dying relative’s life be prolonged.
The big toe is scientifically known as the hallux. Unlike the other four toes, the hallux typically has only two bones instead of three.
Frank Sinatra famously said that “orange is the happiest color,” and that wearing it filled him with confidence and a sense of joy.
When coffee first emerged in the Ottoman Empire in the 16th century, it was considered a drug, and its consumption was forbidden.
In 2010, Abu Dhabi’s Emirates Palace Hotel displayed a $10,000 artificial tree decorated with $11 million worth of jewels, including diamonds and emeralds.
Historically, an “outlaw” was someone who was no longer protected by the law as punishment for a crime, allowing members of the public to persecute them at will legally.
